verdict: Portugal wins on tax (NHR successor) and visa simplicity. Spain wins on city diversity, food and cost outside Madrid/Barcelona.
Lisbon for nomad community and Atlantic surf. Porto for 30% lower rent, river charm, and a slower pace.
Lisbon for sunsets and weather. Madrid for 24-hour life, deeper food scene, and a stronger jobs market.
Barcelona for beach + mountains + tech jobs. Lisbon for cheaper rent, easier visa, and a tighter nomad community.
Bali for beach, yoga, and a polished nomad scene with prices to match. Chiang Mai for half the cost, mountain air, and the original nomad community.
Bali for island life and wellness. Bangkok for world-class internet, 5G, year-round 5-star food at street prices, and a real city.
Bangkok for ambition, networking, and the world's best urban transport. Chiang Mai for cost, calm, and a tighter creator community.
Mexico for diversity (CDMX, beach, colonial cities) and easier US-orbit logistics. Colombia for spring weather year-round (Medellín) and a 2-year DNV.
Mexico City for size, food, and culture depth. Medellín for eternal spring weather, lower cost, and a tighter nomad scene.
Tbilisi for 1-year visa-free, wine, and a small nomad scene. Istanbul for size, history, two-continent thrill and dense coworking.
Thailand for the new DTV 5-year visa and Bangkok infrastructure. Vietnam for 30% lower costs and emerging cities like Da Nang.
Vietnam for cheapest of cheap SEA living. Indonesia (Bali) for the largest nomad community on earth, with the price tag rising.
Greece for islands, cheaper food, and a 50% tax break on DNV income. Croatia for safer cities, faster admin, and zero tax under DNV.
Estonia for e-Residency and company formation. Portugal for weather, lifestyle, lower income threshold.
Panama for USD currency, banking, and tax-friendly residency. Costa Rica for nature, safety, and pura vida calm.
Dubai for tax-free income and a remote work visa anyone can get. Singapore for safety and Asia hub access, but visa is much harder.
Argentina for Buenos Aires energy, wine, and the cheapest peso in years. Uruguay for safety, calm, and a more functional state.
KL for English-everywhere ease and DE Rantau visa. Bangkok for raw scale, infrastructure, and density of community.
Malta for compact island life and EU access. Cyprus for cheaper rent, more space, and warmer winters.
Bulgaria for the cheapest EU capital (Sofia) and beaches. Romania for the fastest internet in Europe (190 Mbps avg).
Barcelona for international scene and tech. Valencia for 30% cheaper rent, beach, and a calmer pace.
Berlin for startup density and creative scene. Lisbon for warmth, ocean and easier visa for non-EU.
Budapest for thermal baths, lower rent, and the underrated nomad gem of Central Europe. Prague for picture-book beauty and stronger tech scene.
Medellín for eternal spring (22°C every day) and a tighter nomad core. Buenos Aires for Paris-level architecture at LATAM prices.
Da Nang for the cheapest beach + fast internet combo in Asia. Bali for the community size and developed nomad infrastructure.
